Environmental Impact Statement, Deep Geothermal Test Well, Geothermal Research Investigations, Imperial Valley, California

The Department of the Interior plans to drill a deep geothermal test well in the East Mesa area of Imperial Valley, California, about 10 miles east of Holtville. This project aims to explore the potential of geothermal resources to produce desalted water and generate electricity. The test well will provide crucial data on steam and brine quality, which could lead to future development. The project involves minimal environmental impact, affecting around 14 acres of desert land. The community and environment could benefit from new water and energy resources, addressing regional shortages.
East Mesa, Imperial Valley, Imperial County, California

The Deep Geothermal Test Well project in Imperial Valley was proposed to explore geothermal resources that could help alleviate water and energy shortages in the region. While there may have been some concerns regarding environmental impacts, the project was designed to have minimal effects on the surrounding desert land. Ultimately, the project aimed to provide valuable data for future geothermal development, which could benefit the local community.

"It is common knowledge that the West, including the country of Mexico, is deficient from a water supply point of view when considering the availability of water from the Colorado River system, only, and any program to develop additional water has to be a benefit." — Imperial Irrigation District

"The proposed drilling operation and clearing of 10 acres will not impact on land use and agriculture, in general and specifically on the work of the Soil Conservation Service." — Soil Conservation Service
